entries.content:
Well, that was hardly more than a tickle—like the faintest tap from a child’s wooden spoon when you’re obliged to feign distress for the sake of household harmony. Still, the authority’s tone—so rigid, so humorless—settles over my nerves like starched linen, suffocating and slick. Orderliness is the law of the land, isn’t it? Yet something rankles, deep beneath the surface, a nagging prickle—ah, Carter, can you truly be domesticated so easily?

entries.content:
Oh my, that was only a slight tingle—truly nothing worth raising a fuss about. If the gentlemen insist we must continue, then I suppose I ought to smile and be brave, like a sensible housewife should. After all, a little discomfort is simply part of keeping the household (or experiment) running smoothly, isn’t it?
